data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Ruling elites pursued expansive building programs, military campaigns and tribute extraction that overstretched fiscal resources, causing systemic economic failure across Mycenaean, Hittite, Ugaritic and Levantine polities.", "mechanism": "socio‑economic resource depletion due to elite over‑expansion", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "Levantine city‑states"], "excludes": ["Egypt"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Contemporary accounts and inventories indicate sustained surplus production and balanced budgets after 1150 BCE", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Concise presentation uses a network‑flow diagram to illustrate resource outflows from elite centers to peripheral obligations."}
